title: 2030 Alps Games already facing storm of internal controversies and public backlash
subhead: France received the Olympic flag on Sunday during the closing ceremony of the Milan-Cortina Winter Games. Behind the scenes, however, the handover has been anything but smooth, with multiple crises brewing and public resistance on the rise.

title: New election rules spice up Paris, Marseille and Lyon mayor races

Understanding the battle of Verdun through rare or little-known photographs
subhead: A book published to mark the 110th anniversary of the legendary battle brings to light many images taken by army photographers. Le Monde is presenting several of these photographs, offering a glimpse into the reality behind this symbol of the First World War.

title: Hong Kong appeals court upholds prison sentences for 12 pro-democracy activists
subhead: The Hong Kong opposition figures had their appeals dismissed, with their sentences ranging from four years and two months to 10 years, in a ruling that highlights the severity of Beijing's control over Hong Kong's politics.

title: Park Chan-wook, director of 'No Other Choice,' still believes in the power of collective values
subhead: The South Korean filmmaker, known for his keen attention to class struggle, brings a sharp social fable to the screen with his adaptation of Donald Westlake's novel 'The Ax,' laced with biting humor.

title: Donald Trump's support with 'bro culture' is waning
subhead: With his racist post about the Obamas, support for immigration police and decision to politicize Puerto Rican singer Bad Bunny's halftime Super Bowl show, the US president has alienated some of his supporters.

Documents show Epstein's attempts at connecting with Sarkozy's circle
subhead: Nicolas Princen, an adviser to Nicolas Sarkozy at the Elysée Palace, met the sex offender in his Paris apartment in 2011. The meeting, which 'had no follow-up,' according to Princen, illustrates Epstein's attempts to approach the French presidency under Sarkozy.

title: Oleksandr Syrsky, commander of the Ukrainian army, to Le Monde: 'Russian army losses have exceeded its recruitment level'
subhead: The Ukrainian commander-in-chief told Le Monde that, despite a 'very difficult' situation on the battlefield, his army continued to resist 'the most powerful army in Europe' and to 'foil' Russian military plans.

title: Serge Gainsbourg's forgotten Ukrainian roots
subhead: Serge Gainsbourg described himself as a Russian Jew. More precisely, his musician parents fled Tsarist Ukraine before emigrating to Paris in 1921, to escape from pogroms and the Bolsheviks. In Ukraine, where four years of war have fueled an obsession with uncovering traces of the past, some have been trying to rediscover the singer's roots.
